name: Tyap
notes: Tyap (a̱lyem Tyap), a Plateau (Atlantic-Congo) language cluster of southern Kaduna State, Nigeria. ORTHOGRAPHY: Latin script. The Tyap Literacy Committee alphabet (Zwunzwuo A̱lyem Tyap ji, drafted in the early 1990s; Follingstad 1992) has 39 letters with an explicit phonetic value for each: a /a/, a̱ /ə/, b, ch /t͡ʃ/, chy /t͡ʃʲ/, d, e, f, g /ɡ/, gb /ɡ͡b/, gh /ɣ/, ghw /ɣʷ/, ghy /ɣʲ/, h, hy /ç/, hw /ʍ/, i, i̱ /ɪ/, j /d͡ʒ/, jhy /ʒʲ/, k, kh /x/, kp /k͡p/, l, m, n, ng /ŋ/, ny /ɲ/, o, p, r, s, sh /ʃ/, shy /ʃʲ/, t, ts /t͡s/, u, v, w, y /j/, z /d͡z/. A 2018 revision reduces the BASIC chart to 24 letters, in which the former digraph ⟨ch⟩ is written ⟨c⟩ (and the palatalised series ⟨cy⟩); both spellings are accepted here (⟨c⟩ = ⟨ch⟩ = /t͡ʃ/, ⟨cy⟩ = ⟨chy⟩ = /t͡ʃʲ/). Gotchas: ⟨z⟩ is /d͡z/, NOT [z]; ⟨kh⟩ = /x/ and ⟨gh⟩ = /ɣ/; ⟨hw⟩ = /ʍ/; ⟨a̱⟩ and ⟨i̱⟩ are the base letter plus COMBINING MACRON BELOW (U+0331), not precomposed characters, so input must be encoded that way. The digraphs cy, gb, gh, ghw, ghy, jhy, kh, kp, ng, ny, sh, shy and ts are single units of sound. Beyond this basic chart the language has 80+ labialised and palatalised consonants with a fortis/lenis contrast; fortis is written by doubling the letter and is NOT modelled here (a doubled letter simply yields a doubled IPA symbol). Vowels are /i ɪ e ə a o u/ (with /ɨ/ in some analyses), short or long, plus diphthongs. INPUT CONTRACT: input is assumed to be the Latin Tyap-proper orthography of the Tyap Literacy Committee (either the 39-letter chart or the 2018 24-letter basic chart), already normalised; the marginal indigenous Akai script is not supported. Tyap is NOT fully standardised — each dialect (Jju, Gworok, Sholyia̱, Tyap proper) runs its own orthography committee — so dialect texts may deviate. TONE: Tyap is a tone language, but the literacy-committee alphabet marks no tone and ordinary text is written WITHOUT tone diacritics. This spec transcribes SEGMENTS ONLY; unwritten tone is NOT RECOVERABLE from the orthography.
